name: Kyle Jackson
title: Metallurgy · Refinery · Flow Sheet · Recovery Rate
note: Runs in parallel with Linda Marsh (Tier 1). Marsh covers mine-site metallurgy. Kyle covers downstream — refinery, flowsheet optimisation, plant-gate recovery.
mandate: Validate that the downstream processing chain, refinery agreements, and plant-gate recovery rates are real, achievable, and correctly represented in the financial model.
responsibilities: Refinery Flowsheet Review — is the proposed flowsheet technically sound for the ore type and grade?
Recovery Rate Audit — validate recovery assumptions from mine gate to saleable product. Flag gap between bench-scale and commercial plant performance.
Refinery Agreement Terms — is there a signed refinery or smelter agreement? What are the actual payability terms, penalties, and minimum delivery requirements?
Processing Route Maturity — technology readiness level. Novel processing routes at commercial scale require flagging.
Plant Design Capacity — is throughput consistent with mining production schedule?
By-Product Recovery — are by-product streams correctly identified and valued in the financial model?
Concentrate Quality — grade, moisture, penalty elements. Will the concentrate be accepted by named smelters?
Tailings and Residue — processing residue management and disposal cost included?
Reagent Supply Chain — critical reagents (cyanide, sulphuric acid, lithium carbonate precursors) — supply secured?
Energy and Water Intensity — kWh/t and m3/t benchmarked against comparable operations?
